title: Gemini Omni Tutorial: How to Use Gemini Omni AI for Beginners
summary: Google's Gemini Omni Flash is a video generation model designed to support iterative, editable video workflows rather than one-shot generation. The tutorial evaluates its performance on physics simulation, character consistency, scene memory, real footage editing, and comparisons with competitors like Sora and Seedance 2.
writeup: This AI Master tutorial introduces Google's Gemini Omni Flash, a video generation model built for iterative, editable workflows instead of one-shot output. The walkthrough tests the model across physics simulation, character consistency, scene memory, and editing of real footage, and sets it against competitors including Sora and Seedance 2. The focus on refinement and memory matters because it targets the consistency and revision gaps that limit single-pass video generation. Key takeaways center on how Omni Flash holds character and scene continuity through successive edits and how its results compare to rival models.
